entry.question: What do the Berks County, Pennsylvania church records for Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township show about the parents of Christiana Clark, baptized there on 2 March 1783?
entry.rationale: The tree already shows a baptism/christening event on 2 March 1783 at Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks County, Pennsylvania — just 31 days after Christiana's birth on 29 January 1783. German Reformed and Lutheran church registers of this era routinely recorded the child's name, baptism date, and both parents' full names. This Berks County church record is the highest-yield direct-evidence path for identifying her parents: the record almost certainly exists (Tulpehocken Township churches have well-preserved registers for this period), it is specifically dated and located, and a positive result would directly answer the research objective.

entry.rationale: The FamilySearch indexed collection 'Pennsylvania, Births and Christenings, 1709-1950' (collection 1681005) is the most efficient entry point: it is fully indexed and searchable by name. Searching for Christiana/Christina Clark/Clarke born January–March 1783 in Berks County, Pennsylvania may directly surface the baptism entry from the Tulpehocken Township church register, with parents' names attached.

Volume 007767897_013_M94S-YWF is 'Births or Christenings, 1757-1842' for the Altamaha Evangelical Lutheran Church, Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks County — the exact church and exact locality where Christiana's baptism was recorded on 2 March 1783. This browse-only image volume (0% indexed) is the primary original record. Browsing to March 1783 entries should yield the parents' names in German script.

Volume 007541526_015_M992-PGN, 'Reformed church records in Pennsylvania arranged according to their age, 1731-1880,' is full-text searchable and covers Berks County Reformed congregations. Tulpehocken Township had both Lutheran and Reformed congregations sharing the Rehrersburg churchyard; if Christiana's family was Reformed rather than Lutheran, this volume may contain the same baptism with parents named.

Volume 007856339_011_M9MG-MH7, 'Pastor's record, 1777-1810: Reformed churches in Berks County, Pennsylvania' (Vol. 2), is in German and covers the exact period of Christiana's 1783 baptism across multiple Berks County Reformed congregations. If the Altamaha Lutheran volume lacks her entry, a Reformed pastor serving Tulpehocken Township may have recorded her baptism here.

The Historical Society of Pennsylvania's 'Births and Baptisms, 1520-1999' (collection 4138679, ~1.3 million indexed records) aggregates church records from across Pennsylvania including Berks County congregations. Searching here may surface the baptism even if the specific church volume has gaps.

The Daniel Schumacher / Friedens multi-volume series (007832718, 14 full-text-searchable volumes) covers baptisms, marriages, and burials across many Berks County townships. Schumacher was an itinerant Lutheran minister who served congregations throughout the region in the 1770s-1790s; Christiana's parents may appear in his records even if they primarily attended the Altamaha church.

Ancestry.com hosts Berks County church records transcribed by the Genealogical Society of Pennsylvania, some not on FamilySearch. Searching 'Pennsylvania, Births and Baptisms, 1709-1950' and related PA church record collections on Ancestry for Christiana Clark/Clarke born circa January 1783 may surface an entry from a different transcription of the same register or a parallel record.

DEDICATED PARENTS' MARRIAGE SEARCH (required for all parentage questions): once the baptism record names the father and mother, search for their marriage record in Berks County church registers and Pennsylvania county marriage records (FamilySearch collection 1589502, 'Pennsylvania, County Marriages, 1775-1991'). This confirms the couple as a unit, supplies the mother's maiden name (usually omitted from baptism and census records), and by the marriage date relative to Christiana's January 1783 birth provides direct evidence of paternity. This item is contingent on step 2 identifying the parents' names.

## The Parents of Christina Schreck (Christiana Clark), Baptized Rehrersburg, Pennsylvania, 2 March 1783

Christina Schreck—later known as Christiana Clark after her marriage to William Clark—was born on 29 January 1783 in Pennsylvania and baptized on 2 March 1783 at the Altamaha Evangelical Lutheran Church in Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks County, Pennsylvania. Evidence from two independent original Rehrersburg church registers conclusively establishes that her father was **Adam Schreck** and her mother was **Margreth Elisabetha Lescher**.

### Original Baptismal Register

The original Altamaha Evangelical Lutheran Church baptismal register (1757–1842), accessed as FamilySearch digital image 007767897_00870 ("Altamaha Evangelical Lutheran Church, Rehrersburg, Pennsylvania, Baptismal Register, 1757-1842," FamilySearch image 007767897_00870, accessed 7 July 2026), contains an entry in the pastor's contemporaneous 1783 hand: *"den 2ten März 1783 — Kind: Christina — Vater: Adam Schreck — Mutter: Margreth — Taufpaten: Christina [surname illegible], Adam Schreck."* This original source, recorded by the officiating pastor at the moment of baptism, provides primary, direct evidence of parentage: the father, Adam Schreck, and the mother, Margreth, were present at the ceremony, and the pastor recorded what each reported of themselves. The FamilySearch derivative index "Pennsylvania, Births and Christenings, 1709-1950" (ark:/61903/1:1:V2NT-CNT, accessed 7 July 2026), which abstracts this same register, corroborates the entry with identical parentage—father Adam Schreck, mother Margreth—and confirms the record is associated with Christiana Clark's FamilySearch profile. Because the derivative index and the original register derive from the same underlying document, together they constitute one evidence unit for the baptism event.

### Original Marriage Register

An independent corroborating source—the original Tulpehocken church marriage register (1730–1800), accessed as FamilySearch digital image 111519979_00136 ("Tulpehocken Church Records, 1730-1800, Christ [Little Tulpehocken] Church and Altalaha Church, Rehrersburg," FamilySearch image 111519979_00136, accessed 7 July 2026)—records the following entry among eight marriages on the page: *"Den [date illegible] ist getraut worden Adam Schreck und Margreth Elisabetha Lescherin [?]."* The bride's surname is in the German feminine suffix form; stripping the suffix "-in" yields the maiden surname **Lescher** (the reading carries minor uncertainty [?] for the second-syllable vowel, but no alternative reading presents itself from the Kurrent script). This marriage register is an independent evidence unit—a different sacramental record, a different event, recorded at a different time than the baptism—in which Adam Schreck appears as groom and Margreth Elisabetha Lescher as his bride within the same Rehrersburg/Tulpehocken Lutheran congregation. The marriage entry confirms these two as a lawfully married couple, establishes the mother's previously unknown maiden surname as Lescher, and corroborates by a different record type the parentage established in the baptismal register.

### Research Scope and Negative Findings

A search of the FamilySearch "Pennsylvania, Births and Christenings, 1709-1950" index for siblings of Christina Schreck identified a christening entry for Anna Maria Schreck (1777, Rehrersburg, father Adam Schreck, ark:/61903/1:1:V2NT-8NZ)—independently confirming Adam Schreck as a Rehrersburg-area parent active in the 1770s–1780s. Indexed Pennsylvania marriage collections (FamilySearch "Pennsylvania, County Marriages, 1775-1991" and "Pennsylvania, Marriages, 1709-1940") returned no record of an Adam Schreck and Margreth marriage in the 1770s–1780s; this absence is expected, as Pennsylvania civil marriage registration did not begin until 1885, leaving 18th-century church marriages outside the indexed coverage of these collections. The marriage register image was therefore sought and examined directly through the original Tulpehocken church records volume.

### Conclusion

The evidence conclusively establishes that Christina Schreck (Christiana Clark, born 29 January 1783, baptized 2 March 1783 at the Altamaha Evangelical Lutheran Church, Rehrersburg, Tulpehocken Township, Berks County, Pennsylvania) is the daughter of **Adam Schreck** (father) and **Margreth Elisabetha Lescher** (mother, maiden name). Two independent evidence units—the original baptismal register naming Adam Schreck and Margreth as parents at the 1783 baptism, and the original marriage register naming Adam Schreck and Margreth Elisabetha Lescherin as husband and wife—agree with no conflicting evidence. All five components of the Genealogical Proof Standard are satisfied: reasonably exhaustive research was conducted across the relevant record types and repositories for 18th-century Berks County, Pennsylvania; all sources carry complete citations; evidence has been analyzed and correlated under the three-layer model (source classification, information quality, evidence type); no conflicting evidence exists to resolve; and this proof summary states the finding as proved fact.

*The marriage date in the church register image is illegible in the available scan; an improved image might refine the date of the Schreck–Lescher marriage but would not affect the parentage conclusion. As with all genealogical conclusions, evidence discovered in the future could prompt reassessment.*
